questions.a: It forces buffered file data and metadata to reach durable storage.

questions.q: What is the key advantage of SSDs over HDDs?
questions.a: Much lower access latency and no mechanical seeks.
Kernel memory used to cache file data between storage and applications.

questions.q: What does sequential I/O usually optimize for?
questions.a: Throughput and fewer seeks or random flash writes.

questions.q: Why is random I/O often slower than sequential I/O?
questions.a: Random access defeats prefetching and forces more device seeks or flash page updates.

questions.q: Why does fsync matter for databases?
questions.a: It protects committed data from being lost if the OS or machine crashes.
When a small logical write causes much larger physical write work below it.

questions.q: Why do file systems use journaling or log structures?
questions.a: To recover consistent metadata and sometimes data after crashes.

questions.q: What is the difference between latency and throughput for storage?
questions.a: Latency is per-operation delay; throughput is total work completed per unit time.
It bypasses the page cache for workloads that manage their own buffering or want predictable behavior.

questions.q: When would you prefer an append-only design over random in-place updates?
questions.a: When you want simpler crash recovery, higher write throughput, or lower write amplification.

questions.q: When is it worth paying the cost of fsync on every write?
questions.a: When data loss is unacceptable and the workload can tolerate the latency.

questions.q: How do you choose between RAID and application-level replication?
questions.a: RAID protects a disk set locally; replication protects availability across failures and is usually a system-level decision.

questions.q: Why can a page cache help and hurt at the same time?
questions.a: It speeds repeated reads, but it can also hide true memory pressure or duplicate buffering.

questions.q: What tradeoff does stronger consistency on storage updates introduce?
questions.a: It usually reduces throughput because the system must wait for durable confirmation more often.

questions.q: Why can smaller random writes age SSDs faster?
questions.a: Flash pages must be erased and rewritten in larger blocks, so small updates create extra internal copying.
